summary: Monster Playin' In The Band jam, tight Samson opener, strong drums
blurb: The show shines with a thunderous Playin' In The Band stretch and a fiery Samson & Delilah opener. Jerry’s guitar and the dual‑drum attack drive highlights like China Doll, Victim Or The Crime > Crazy Fingers, and a soaring Liberty encore.
review: May 26, 1993 at Cal Expo captured one of the late‑era Dead’s most exhilarating outings. From the opening smash of Samson & Delilah, where Vince adds a gritty vocal bite, the band rolls into a set that balances deep cuts (Here Comes Sunshine, Broken Arrow) with fan favorites. The second set erupts after Box of Rain into a seamless Victim Or The Crime → Crazy Fingers → Playing In The Band suite that blossoms into an almost 30‑minute jam, punctuated by a melodic drum showcase and a soaring Corinna reprise before collapsing back into a heartfelt China Doll and a rollicking Around & Around. The encore’s Liberty caps the night with a jubilant sing‑along. Across all recordings the performance is uniformly praised – Jerry’s guitar shreds and wails, Phil’s bass provides both punch and nuance (even if some listeners note thin low end), Bob anchors the rhythm with solid vocals, Vince’s keys blend into the jam fabric, and Mickey & Bill deliver an aggressive, melodic dual‑drum assault that lifts the whole show. For anyone hunting a late‑90s classic that feels like a throwback to the band’s ’72 monster jams, this is a must‑listen.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All six recordings carry a positive sentiment, repeatedly praising the Playin' In The Band jam, Samson opener, and overall band cohesion. Multiple sources highlight the same songs (Playin', China Doll, Victim/Crazy Fingers) and describe Jerry’s guitar as "smoky" and "shredding", drums as "melodic" and "aggressive", confirming strong consensus of excellence.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with good clarity; captures the monster Playin' In The Band jam and Samson opener cleanly, offering the most complete and balanced listening experience.

key_highlights: Monster Playin' In The Band jam spanning drums‑space‑Corinna and back
Powerful Samson & Delilah opener with Vince’s vocal contribution
Tight, emotive China Doll performance and reprise
Melodic dual‑drum showcase that drives the second set

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers smoky, fast, edgy shredding on Samson and a sweet, fluid tone throughout the Playin' In The Band marathon, often highlighted as a standout moment.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh’s bass cuts through with punch and nuanced string work, though some listeners note thin low‑end that benefits from EQ.
band_performance.Bob: Weir provides solid rhythm guitar and vocal pushes; occasional hoarseness is mentioned but overall his contributions are steady and supportive.
band_performance.Vince: Welnick’s keyboards blend into the jam fabric, adding texture without disappearing; his vocals on Samson receive specific praise, though one review mentions his tone wasn’t to everyone’s liking.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart delivers aggressive, melodic drumming that drives the extended jams and is repeatedly called a highlight of the night.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann matches Hart’s energy with powerful fills; together they form a dual‑drum engine that propels the second set.

must_listen_sequences: Stuck Inside of Mobile with the Memphis Blues Again
